title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Lymph Node Swelling
intro: Herbal teas for lymph node swelling, also known as lymphatic herbal teas, refer to a type of herbal remedy used to treat swelling and inflammation of the lymph nodes. These teas work by promoting lymphatic drainage, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system, which can help to alleviate symptoms of swollen lymph nodes. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat this ailment include dandelion root tea, which acts as a diuretic to help remove excess fluids from the body; peppermint tea, which reduces inflammation and eases pain; echinacea tea, which boosts the immune system to fight off underlying infections; ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes lymphatic drainage; licorice root tea, which soothes and calms the lymphatic system; and sage tea, which reduces inflammation and improves lymphatic function. By incorporating these herbal teas into a treatment plan, individuals may experience relief from swollen lymph nodes and improve overall health.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, also known as lymphadenitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and glycosides, help to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating the symptoms of lymphadenitis. The herbal preparation also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect the body from oxidative stress and promote tissue healing. By reducing inflammation and supporting the immune system, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to treat lymph node swelling and promote overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove.
Add the 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea or stomach upset. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat this ailment, take precautions by monitoring your body's reaction and starting with low doses to avoid interactions with other medications, as well as avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root per cup
Take the tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvements
Combine with a healthy diet and regular exercise for best results
Consult a healthcare professional before long-term usage or high dosages

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used to treat lymph node swelling ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, thereby aiding in the treatment of lymph node swelling. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which contributes to the alleviation of lymph node swelling. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals may experience relief from lymph node swelling and other associated symptoms, such as pain and discomfort, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to help reduce lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate lymph node swelling symptoms, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your body's reaction, and be cautious of interactions with other medications, as it may also cause bleeding or allergic reactions in rare cases.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba per cup of tea.
Increase dosage to 1/2 teaspoon per cup after 2-3 days.
Take tea 30 minutes before meals to enhance absorption.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 2-4 weeks to see results.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, also known as lymphadenitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing swelling and pain, as well as promoting the recovery of the lymphatic system. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin and flavonoids, in Silybum marianum teas help to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increase the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thus alleviating the symptoms of lymphadenitis. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat lymph node swelling include reduced swelling and pain, improved lymphatic function, and a lower risk of complications such as infection or abscess formation.
preparation_procedure: Gather Silybum marianum seeds and dried flowers. Use 1 tablespoon of seeds and 1 teaspoon of flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Silybum marianum seeds and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for lymph node swelling relief. You can add honey for taste.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to potentially treat lymph node swelling, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, as it may exacerbate digestive problems, and to be aware of potential cross-contamination with other substances during preparation.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Silybum marianum tea.
Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue usage for 1-2 weeks or as advised by your healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, a common symptom of various health conditions. The anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and promote the elimination of excess fluids, thereby alleviating lymph node swelling. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. By using Taraxacum officinale teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and non-invasive treatment option that promotes lymphatic function and overall health.

Chop the leaves and flowers finely and place them in a clean cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the chopped Taraxacum officinale.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3 times a day to help reduce lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small amounts, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding consumption if you experience any adverse reactions, as excessive use can lead to interactions with other medications or worsen underlying health conditions.

Drink 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.

Continue using Taraxacum officinale tea for 2-4 weeks for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas, derived from the bark of the Cinchona tree, have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as quinine and alkaloids, help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause infection. By reducing inflammation and killing off pathogens, Cinchona officinalis teas help to promote the healing process and alleviate the discomfort associated with swollen lymph nodes. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat lymph node swelling include reduced inflammation, faster recovery times, and a lower risk of complications, making it a popular natural remedy among those seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1 teaspoon of Cinchona officinalis bark, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Cinchona officinalis bark. Let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the bark.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but using them may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to their high quinine content. When using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to take precautions by drinking plenty of water, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring blood pressure levels closely, as they can exacerbate existing cardiovascular conditions and interact with certain medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Cinchona officinalis tea.
Start with 1/4 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea twice a day.
Gradually increase to 1 cup of tea three times a day.
Drink tea after meals, ideally with 1 tablespoon of honey.
Continue for 2-4 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen lymph nodes. The active compounds in ginger tea also exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which aid in regulating the immune system's response to infections and promoting overall health. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas has been shown to provide relief from lymph node swelling and other related symptoms, making it a valuable natural remedy for promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Wash the ginger root under cold water, then peel it using a vegetable peeler.
Cut the peeled ginger into small pieces and place them in the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginger in the infuser or cup, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey to taste if desired. Drink 2-3 cups of ginger tea per day for relief from lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas may have potential benefits in treating lymph node swelling due to the presence of lariciresinol, a phytocompound that was found to inhibit efflux pump activity in Salmonella typhimurium, potentially leading to effective killing of drug-resistant bacteria at lower concentrations.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to potentially alleviate lymph node swelling, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, users should be cautious of interactions with medications, monitor their body's response, and start with small doses to gradually increase as needed.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Consume Zingiber officinale tea for 7-10 consecutive days.
Monitor lymph node swelling and consult doctor if no improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, also known as lymphadenitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system to combat infections that may cause lymph node swelling. These herbal preparations help to treat lymph node swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which in turn helps to reduce swelling and promote healing. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat lymph node swelling include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and a shorter recovery time, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste. Mix well.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day. You can also store it in the fridge for later use.
Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before consuming Echinacea angustifolia tea regularly, especially if you have any medical conditions or allergies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling ailment, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, which can be relieved by discontinuing its use. Precautions should be taken when using Echinacea angustifolia teas, such as starting with small doses, avoiding its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and monitoring blood pressure levels due to potential interactions with medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/4 cup of Echinacea angustifolia tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per 1 cup of boiling water.

Consume Echinacea angustifolia tea for 4-6 weeks continuously.
Monitor lymph node swelling and adjust usage as needed with doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the lymph nodes. By modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas aid in the resolution of lymph node swelling and promote overall well-being. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for this ailment include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced overall health, making it a valuable natural remedy for lymph node swelling.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Cut 2 tablespoons of the roots into small pieces and put them in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop. Let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Pour the boiled water over the root pieces in the cup or teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and drink it while it's still warm. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mini reviews in medicinal chemistry", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may have potential in managing respiratory infections, including those with lymph node swelling, due to its pharmacological actions of modulating the immune system, inhibiting virus growth, producing anti-inflammatory activity, and inactivating viruses.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used as a treatment for lymph node swelling, but it may cause side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and low potassium levels due to its licorice root content. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation and avoid consuming them for extended periods, as well as to limit the intake of sodium and alcohol while using this herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day
Use 1-2 tablespoons of dried root per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink
Continue for 1-2 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional

The bioactive constituents of Arctium lappa, including inulin, saponins, and phenolic acids, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to reduce swelling and promote the resolution of lymphadenitis. The benefits of using Arctium lappa teas for lymph node swelling include reduced pain and swelling, improved lymphatic function, and enhanced overall immune system health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Arctium lappa roots and leaves. Wash them in cold water.
Dry the roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried Arctium lappa roots and leaves with 4 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 3 times a day, for 1-2 weeks to help reduce lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arctium lappa teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea or stomach upset, and allergic reactions, such as hives or itching. To avoid adverse effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to monitor for any signs of allergic reactions or gastrointestinal distress, and to discontinue use if they occur.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizome of the turmeric plant, have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, also known as lymphadenitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with this ailment. The bioactive constituents, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to the therapeutic effects of Curcuma longa teas. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been found to be beneficial in treating lymph node swelling, as it helps to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and prevent further complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Curcuma longa root powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed, up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Anti-cancer agents in medicinal chemistry, Curcuma longa teas may potentially alleviate lymph node swelling through its bioactive immunomodulator, curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating lymph node swelling, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as interactions with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming it in large amounts, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding, and to be cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, also known as cranberry teas, have been used to treat lymph node swelling, a condition that can be caused by bacterial infections such as lymphadenitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, particularly the presence of flavonoids and anthocyanins, help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with lymph node inflammation. The bioactive constituents, including proanthocyanidins, help to prevent bacterial adhesion to the bladder and urinary tract walls, thereby reducing the risk of infection and subsequent lymph node swelling. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can provide relief from lymph node swelling and promote overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Vaccinium macrocarpon (Cranberry) flowers and leaves.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Vaccinium macrocarpon into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Vaccinium macrocarpon in the tea infuser or cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Vaccinium macrocarpon teas safely, it is recommended to consume them in moderation, avoid combining them with other medications, and monitor blood sugar levels closely, as excessive consumption may exacerbate pre-existing conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea pallida teas have been traditionally used to treat lymph node swelling, a condition characterized by inflammation and pain in the lymph nodes. The anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties of Echinacea pallida teas help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea pallida teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, play a crucial role in modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation. By using Echinacea pallida teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea pallida flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Combine the Echinacea pallida flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired. Stir well to dissolve.
Drink the Echinacea pallida tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plants (Basel, Switzerland), Echinacea pallida teas may have immunomodulatory effects, but there is no specific information in the given study about its use for lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea pallida teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions include being aware of the potential for increased heart rate and blood pressure, avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and starting with low doses to monitor for adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as dong quai, have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat lymph node swelling, a condition often associated with infection, inflammation, or cancer. The teas' anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms, promoting a healthy recovery. The bioactive constituents, including ferulic acid, isoflavones, and lignans, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to their therapeutic effects. By consuming Angelica sinensis teas, individuals can benefit from improved lymphatic function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall immune system function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for treating lymph node swelling.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica sinensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Angelica sinensis root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Angelica sinensis root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root. You can add honey to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to help reduce lymph node swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Angelica sinensis teas are associated with significant adverse effects, usually in the form of a hypersensitivity reaction.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can be used to treat lymph node swelling, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and nausea due to its blood-thinning properties and potential interactions with medications. To use Angelica sinensis teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring blood pressure, and avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to its potential effects on the fetus and infant.

preparation_study: According to Plant foods for human nutrition (Dordrecht, Netherlands), Echinacea purpurea teas are not mentioned as being studied for lymph node swelling in the provided study, which primarily examined the clinical efficacy and safety of various herbal teas for other health conditions, such as female health, diabetes, heart disease, and weight loss.
intro_study: According to "Complementary therapies in medicine", teas for lymph node swelling may be effective in reducing lymphedema burden, with some studies suggesting that herbal treatments, particularly those containing coumarin, can help reduce edema volume and improve symptoms.
